questions.q: What is the reaction of phenol with chloroform in the presence of aqueous NaOH?
questions.answer: Phenol reacts with chloroform in the presence of aqueous NaOH to form salicylaldehyde.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The best answer to distinguish between methanol and ethanol is _______.
Explain why phenols are more acidic than alcohols.
questions.answer: Phenols are more acidic than alcohols due to resonance stabilization of the phenoxide ion.

questions.q: State whether the statement is True or False: Phenol is less acidic than ethanol.
What is the product formed when ethyl magnesium bromide reacts with methanol?
Which of the following is NOT a property of phenols? (A) acidic, (B) basic, (C) soluble in water, (D) volatile
Describe the Victor Meyer test for distinguishing between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols.
questions.answer: The Victor Meyer test involves the reaction of the alcohol with red phosphorus and iodine, followed by addition of sodium hydroxide and heating. Primary alcohols give a red color, secondary alcohols give a blue color, and tertiary alcohols give no color.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The reaction of ether with HI is an example of _______.
State the difference between alcohols and phenols with respect to their acidity.
questions.answer: Alcohols are neutral, while phenols are acidic due to the resonance stabilization of the phenoxide ion.

questions.q: Which of the following alcohols will give a positive iodoform test? (A) methanol, (B) ethanol, (C) propan-2-ol, (D) all of the above
